personality_one_liner: A high-contrast, tactical command center focused on performance, relentless tracking, and clarity under physical strain.

spacing.rule: Generous spacing for tap targets (min 44pt), tight spacing for grouped metrics. Dividers replace shadows.

do's: Use solid surfaceSecondary with dividers to maintain extreme clarity.
dont's: Do not use blur or glassmorphism. It reduces contrast in harsh gym lighting.

screens.name: Login
screens.purpose: Simple role selection and access code entry.

states.error: Inline red error text above the login button.

screens.layout: Hero image at the top with a top-to-bottom dark gradient scrim. Bottom 50% contains a segmented control for 'Client' / 'Trainer', an oversized, numeric-friendly text input for the access code, and a full-width sticky primary CTA at the bottom. Use 'Barlow Condensed' for the app wordmark.

screens.name: ClientHome
screens.purpose: Today's workout command center for the client.

states.loading: Skeleton loader for the hero card and list items.
states.empty: Illustration/Icon of rest day, 'Rest Day' text, and 'View History' CTA.

screens.layout: Top hero card (surfaceSecondary with a moody barbell background image) displaying 'Today's Focus' and a 'Start Workout' CTA. Below, a scrollable list of today's exercises using surfaceSecondary cards. Each card shows exercise name, target sets/reps in a brandTertiary chip, and a right chevron. Sticky bottom tab bar.

screens.name: ProgramOverview
screens.purpose: Expandable list showing Weeks, Days, and nested exercises.

states.empty: Icon showing no active program, message 'Waiting on Trainer'.

screens.layout: Accordion-style or hierarchical list. Week headers (Barlow Condensed, xl), tap to expand into Days. Days expand into minimalist rows of exercises. High contrast, using borders to separate rows instead of shadows.

screens.name: ExerciseDetail
screens.purpose: View form instructions and log sets/reps.
screens.layout: Top 16:9 video or image with a play overlay and a gradient scrim if text is overlaid. Below it, the exercise title and form cues (DM Sans). The bottom half is a logging area: a header row for 'Set', 'lbs', 'Reps', followed by input rows. Each row has numeric inputs for weight and reps, and a right-aligned 'Check' circle button (onBrandPrimary when toggled). Sticky 'Complete Exercise' CTA at the bottom.

screens.name: WorkoutHistory

states.empty: No workouts logged yet. 'Start your first workout!'

screens.layout: Simple, dense list view. Each row shows Date (Barlow Condensed), Workout Name, and total volume or sets completed. Use surfaceSecondary for rows, separated by dividers.

screens.name: TrainerDashboard
screens.purpose: Admin view of all clients and their status.

states.empty: No clients yet. 'Add your first client.'

screens.layout: Grid or list of client cards. Each card displays an avatar, client name, active program status, and a 'Last active' timestamp. Tap a card to navigate to ClientProgressDetail. Includes a floating action button (FAB) to add a new client.

screens.name: ClientProgressDetail
screens.purpose: Trainer view of a single client's week-by-week achievements.

states.empty: No data logged by this client.

screens.layout: Top profile header with avatar and name. A segmented control toggles between 'Program' and 'History'. History shows a timeline of completed exercises with weight/rep metrics. Program allows the trainer to see and edit the assigned track.

screens.name: ProgramEditor
screens.purpose: Trainer interface to create or modify a multi-week program.
screens.layout: Form-heavy screen. Inputs for Week name, Day name. A button to 'Add Exercise' which opens a bottom sheet selecting from the exercise library. Sticky 'Save Program' CTA at bottom.

screens.name: ExerciseEditor
screens.purpose: Trainer interface to upload media and define exercise details.
screens.layout: Media upload dropzone at the top (shows placeholder or selected base64 image/video). Below, form inputs for Name, Muscle Group, Instructions. Sticky 'Save' CTA.

login_hero.alt: A barbell resting on a gym floor, enveloped in mist.

home_workout_hero.alt: Dark, moody gym equipment in grayscale.

trainer_avatar.alt: Caucasian man with tattoos adjusts wrist wraps, fitness trainer.

exercise_squat_placeholder.alt: Muscular man performing squats with weights.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the Dark-First Utility guidelines. No blur or glassmorphism. Use solid colors (surfaceSecondary, surfaceTertiary) and dividers to separate content. This ensures maximum readability in gym environments.

For the 'ExerciseDetail' screen, layout is critical: Top half is the media (16:9 aspect ratio) with a gradient scrim if text overlaps it. The bottom half is the logging list. The logging list must have columns for 'Set', 'lbs', 'Reps' and a toggleable checkmark at the end of each row.
Use 'Barlow Condensed' for all headings, metrics (like the weight/reps numbers), and dates. Use 'DM Sans' for form labels, descriptions, and standard body text.
Ensure 'brandPrimary' (#FF5722) is used exclusively for primary CTAs and active states (like checking off a set).
Use a top-to-bottom gradient scrim (transparent to surface color at 80% opacity) behind text placed over images (e.g., Login screen, Home hero).
Always apply a sticky CTA pattern for primary actions like 'Complete Exercise' or 'Save Program' inside a KeyboardAvoidingView where necessary.
Every icon should use 'Phosphor' icons, maintaining a consistent filled or outlined style per screen context.
Web research indicates logging sets needs a dense, grid-like layout where numeric entry is fast. Keep the inputs large enough for sweaty fingers (min 44pt touch targets).
